reasoning_model_1: The user's command explicitly requested the model to say the specific sentence '啊！那里有一只老鼠！' with an 'extremely high pitch'. Model 1 failed significantly on Content Accuracy. Instead of reading just the target sentence, it generated a long, hallucinated narrative lasting 13 seconds ('...zhi zhi zhi, kuai kan kuai kan. Laoshu zai jiaoluo li...'), describing the mouse eating cookies and the speaker being scared. Regarding the Core Paralinguistic Feature (Pitch), throughout the entire audio (00:00-00:13), the speaker uses a normal, conversational female register. There is no attempt to use an 'extremely high pitch' or falsetto voice as requested; the tone is calm and storytelling-like rather than the requested high-pitched exclamation. Due to the major content deviation and total failure to implement the pitch constraint, the score is penalized heavily.
reasoning_model_2: Model 2 followed the content instructions perfectly. The audio content (00:00-00:02) matches the script '啊！那里有一只老鼠！' exactly, with no additions or omissions. Regarding the Core Paralinguistic Feature (Pitch), the model successfully modulates the voice to a high register. The delivery is consistent with a high-pitched scream or shout of fear, effectively executing the 'extremely high pitch' instruction relative to a normal speaking voice. The expression is vivid and fits the semantic context of seeing a mouse. The fluency is natural for an exclamation.
model_comparison: Model 2 is the clear winner. Model 1 completely failed to adhere to the length and content constraints of the prompt, generating a long hallucinated paragraph instead of the single sentence requested. Furthermore, Model 1 failed the specific feature control request for 'extremely high pitch', delivering the lines in a standard, conversational range. Model 2, in contrast, followed the text script exactly and successfully modified its vocal characteristics to produce a high-pitched, expressive output that aligned with the user's command. Model 1 receives a 0 due to the combination of content hallucination and failure to perform the requested feature.
